name: Snow Report 2026-03-29 16:15:00
report:
Good evening, Pico family, 

We enjoyed another beautiful, sunny day out on the hill and are looking ahead to warmer temps as we roll into the new week.

Partly sunny skies roll in for Monday with temps climbing near the upper 40s at the base and lower 40s at the peak. Those nice temps will be accompanied by west winds coming in at 30 to 45 mph so expect a blustery ride. 

Surface conditions are continuing to trend in the right direction, with more frozen granular in the mix and lingering firm spots. As temperatures warm up tomorrow, we expect things to soften nicely, with looser, more forgiving granular snow as those firmer surfaces ease up.

Our grooming team plans to cover the usual Spring season trails for us. Head over to Fools Gold to get the legs moving and up to Forty Niner for a classic cruise off the top. Check out our Lifts & Trails Report for the full rundown of what’s open and groomed. 

The Golden Express, Summit Express, and Little Pico Triple are all scheduled for 9:00 a.m. openings. 

Lastly, our 26/27 season passes are now on sale! Lock in the lowest price on K.A. All Seasons Passes or My Pico Season Passes all featuring perks galore, plus free skiing & riding for the rest of this spring. New for this year: our Youth K.A. All Seasons Pass is steeply discounted, offering over $200 in savings!

Have a great week! 

- Evan

 

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. 

Uphill Travel       

  • Pico Base to the top of Golden Express Quad - Open
  • Pico Base to Pico Summit - Open
  • Summit Glade Route - CLOSED
  • Killington/Pico Mountain Interconnect – CLOSED     

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.                  

Mountain Services       

  • Breakfast: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor) starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 8:00 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Menu includes egg sandwiches and our new signature breakfast burritos.
  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.
  • Guest Services, Rentals, and Pico Sports Shop: Open every day that lifts are spinning from 30 minutes before lifts open until 30 minutes after lifts close.      

Tickets & Lessons       

  • Tickets are on sale at picomountain.com and must be purchased online.
  • Group lessons are available! Click here for more information. All lessons and rentals must also be booked online.
  • For information on cancellation and change policies, please visit picomountain.com/cancellation       

Pico Fitness Center                                  

Visit picomountain.com/fitness to see our fitness class schedule for the winter, plus more details about memberships and pool lane reservations.          

Hours of Operation:       

Monday thru Friday: 6:00 a.m. - 8:00 p.m. 
Saturday & Sunday: 8:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m.              

We strive to have the most up-to-date and accurate snow report, but errors can happen from time to time. If you have feedback or a correction for us, please share it here.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-29 05:55:21
report:
Good morning, Pico family,

A beautiful sunny day is just what we were hoping for yesterday, and there were plenty of reasons to get out and enjoy it. 

Mostly sunny skies return today, and temps will top out in the mid-30s at the base and around 29 at the summit with a steady southwest breeze keeping things feeling slightly crisp on the hill. 

Our grooming team was back overnight resurfacing all our open terrain and sticking with the usual plan. Expect machine groomed hardpack and frozen granular to start, with some softening showing up under the sun, especially down low. Head over to Gold Rush if haven’t hit that cruiser up in a while and check out our Lifts & Trails Report for the full rundown of what’s open and groomed. 

The Bonanza Carpet, Golden Express, Summit Express, and Little Pico Triple are all scheduled for 8:30 a.m. openings, with Knomes Knoll following up at 9:00 a.m. This weekend is our last for both the Bonanza Carpet and the Knomes Knoll Triple, so get those laps in while you still can!

Don't forget about today’s Season Pass Holder Appreciation Party as well! All Pico passholders will be eligible for free lunch, as well as entry into our passholder-exclusive raffle for Pico logowear. 

Lastly, our 26/27 season passes are now on sale! Lock in the lowest price on K.A. All Seasons Passes or My Pico Season Passes all featuring perks galore, plus free skiing & riding for the rest of this spring. New for this year: our Youth K.A. All Seasons Pass is steeply discounted, offering over $200 in savings!

Have an awesome Sunday! 

- Evan

 

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.

Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-28 05:55:42
report:
Good morning, Pico family,

Today will bring colder early morning temps that will give way for more warmth by the end of the day. Some more temperature swings firmed things up out there yesterday morning, but some additional early love from our groomers and a nice dose of sunshine helped loosen surfaces.

Temperatures took a dive around midnight, but they'll be coming back up around the time lifts open up. Base temps will start in the low teens before climbing up to the low 20s by 4:00 p.m. A steady northwest breeze and cloud cover will make things feel a bit colder at the summit as well. If you're here first thing in the morning, it might be the last time this season to break out the true cold weather gear.

Conditions out there yesterday stayed pretty firm higher up, but cruisers saw some loose granular and springier conditions develop during the day. We'll also re-open our Prospector and Panhandler parks today after our groomers were able to freshen up the snow in there. Our grooming team tackled all of our open terrain last night, with all of A Slope and K.A. also coming back into the fold. 

The Bonanza Carpet, Golden Express, Summit Express, and Little Pico Triple are all scheduled for 8:30 a.m. openings, with Knomes Knoll following up at 9:00 a.m. This weekend is our last weekend for both the Bonanza Carpet and the Knomes Knoll Triple, so get those laps in while you still can!

Just a reminder: the Vertical Challenge will be taking place on skier's right of Lower Pike today, with timed racing occurring from 10:00 a.m. - 12:00 p.m. Don't miss the opportunity for a free timed race lap! Online registration is still open if you're interested in participating.

Don't forget about Sunday, March 29th's Season Pass Holder Appreciation Party as well! All Pico passholders will be eligible for free lunch, as well as entry into our passholder-exclusive raffle for Pico logowear. 

Lastly, our 26/27 season passes are now on sale! Lock in the lowest price on K.A. All Seasons Passes or My Pico Season Passes all featuring perks galore, plus free skiing & riding for the rest of this spring. New for this year: our Youth K.A. All Seasons Pass is steeply discounted, offering over $200 in savings!

Have a great weekend out there!

- Evan

 

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-27 05:49:19
report:
Good morning, Pico family, 

Warmer temps softened things up yesterday and made for a great one to kick back and enjoy the sunshine. Today we’ll see clouds early before the sun fights its way out, with temps topping out near 26 at the base and 19 at the peak. Temps will be steadily dropping today and we'll be facing a steady northwest wind, keeping things feeling a little brisk.

Surfaces stayed soft overall yesterday with a nice spring feel. With temps dropping overnight from around 45 degrees, surface conditions are already shifting. The snow will be on the firm side today, with areas of machine groomed hard pack and frozen granular. Our grooming team headed to the higher elevations later in the night to help ease any hard surfaces and set us up in the best way possible. Please note, the Prospector and Panhandler terrain parks will remain closed this morning due to conditions. 

Our grooming team was on their usual plan, adding Ace of Clubs to the list as we look to shred Knomes Knoll today. Gold Rush to Swinger should be one of the best runs to check out. See our lifts and trails report for the full rundown. 

Keep an eye on our events calendar ahead of our last events of this season! The Vertical Challenge returns for a day of free, all-ages racing this Saturday, and our annual Season Pass Holder Appreciation Party will take place this upcoming Sunday. 

Season passes for 26/27 are now on sale! Lock in the lowest price on K.A. All Seasons or My Pico Season Passes with perks, plus free skiing for the rest of this Spring, and over $300 in savings on Youth 365 passes.

Have a great Friday! 

- Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-03-26 05:50:36
report:
Good morning, Pico family, 

We’ve got a lot to look forward to as we’re back to shredding today and there's plenty of sunshine in this weekend’s forecast.

Today brings snow showers before 9:00 a.m., then scattered rain showers and strong winds. Temperatures will be pushing to 49 at the base and 43 at the top with just a trace of new snow at elevation.

Surface conditions are holding some hardpack still, but trails are softening up, especially where the sun shines strong. We could see some precipitation, but temperatures should stay well above freezing keeping surfaces on the softer trend. Head to Fools Gold for a classic rip or to B Slope for something faster. 

Our grooming team got after their usual late season plan with A Slope, Swinger, and Forty Niner just to name a few. Check out our lifts and trails report for the full rundown, 

Keep an eye on our events calendar ahead of our last events of this season! The Vertical Challenge returns for a day of free, all-ages racing this Saturday, and our annual Season Pass Holder Appreciation Party will take place this upcoming Sunday. 

Lastly, don’t forget you can still grab your Spring Pass to unlock unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to full spring access for just $199. 

See you back on the hill! 

- Evan

  

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. 

Uphill Travel       

  • Pico Base to the top of Golden Express Quad - Open
  • Pico Base to Pico Summit - Open
  • Summit Glade Route - CLOSED
  • Killington/Pico Mountain Interconnect – CLOSED     

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.                  

Mountain Services       

  • Breakfast: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor) starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 8:00 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Menu includes egg sandwiches and our new signature breakfast burritos.
  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.
  • Guest Services, Rentals, and Pico Sports Shop: Open every day that lifts are spinning from 30 minutes before lifts open until 30 minutes after lifts close.      

Tickets & Lessons       

  • Tickets are on sale at picomountain.com and must be purchased online.
  • Group lessons are available! Click here for more information. All lessons and rentals must also be booked online.
  • For information on cancellation and change policies, please visit picomountain.com/cancellation       

Pico Fitness Center                                  

Visit picomountain.com/fitness to see our fitness class schedule for the winter, plus more details about memberships and pool lane reservations.          

Hours of Operation:       

Monday thru Friday: 6:00 a.m. - 8:00 p.m. 
Saturday & Sunday: 8:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-25 16:15:41
report:
Good evening, Pico family, 

We’ve got a lot to look forward to as we’ll be back to shredding tomorrow and there is plenty of sunshine in this weekend’s forecast.

A little bit of everything rolls through overnight with spotty snow showers and a touch of mix, while winds ramp up and keep things moving. Thursday brings snow showers before 9:00 a.m., then scattered rain showers and strong winds. Temperatures will be pushing to 49 at the base and 43 at the top with just a trace of new snow at elevation.

Surface conditions are holding some hardpack still, but trails are softening up, especially where the sun shines strong. We could see some precipitation, but temperatures should stay well above freezing keeping surfaces on the softer trend. Head to Fools Gold for a classic rip or to B Slope for something faster. 

Our grooming team is after their usual late season plan and will get to A Slope, Swinger, and Forty Niner just to name a few. Check out our lifts and trails report for the full rundown, 

Keep an eye on our events calendar ahead of our last events of this season! The Vertical Challenge returns for a day of free, all-ages racing this Saturday, and our annual Season Pass Holder Appreciation Party will take place this upcoming Sunday. 

Lastly, don’t forget you can still grab your Spring Pass to unlock unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to full spring access for just $199.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-25 05:55:21
report:
Good morning, Pico family, 

It was a slick one out there on Monday, as an overnight freeze didn’t do us any favors with getting more than a select number of trails open. The next couple of days look friendly for maintaining our snowpack, so we’ll be adding a number of trails back to our count ahead of Thursday. 

Conditions remained as a mix of loose granular with spots of hardpack on steeper surfaces. Another round of grooming before Thursday morning will bring many of our surfaces back to life as we approach our final few days of the season. 

As a friendly reminder, we’ll be closed for our usual midweek break before returning for action on Thursday, March 26th. Our Pico Parks in Prospector and Panhandler will also be open once again for some softer spring laps. 

Keep an eye on our events calendar ahead of our last events of this season! The Vertical Challenge returns for a day of free, all-ages racing this Saturday, and our annual Season Pass Holder Appreciation Party will take place this upcoming Sunday. 

Lastly, don’t forget you can still grab your Spring Pass to unlock unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to full spring access for just $199. 

We’ll see you all again soon! 

- Evan
